1	#pragma once
2	/**
3	 * @file
4	 * @author  Alex Singer
5	 * @date    January 2025
6	 * @brief   The declaration of the greedy candidate selector class which selects
7	 *          candidate molecules to pack into the given cluster. This class also
8	 *          maintains the gains of packing molecules into clusters.
9	 */
10	
11	#include <map>
12	#include <unordered_set>
13	#include <vector>
14	#include "flat_placement_types.h"
15	#include "logical_ram_infer.h"
16	#include "attraction_groups.h"
17	#include "cluster_legalizer.h"
18	#include "greedy_clusterer.h"
19	#include "logic_types.h"
20	#include "physical_types.h"
21	#include "prepack.h"
22	#include "vtr_ndmatrix.h"
23	#include "vtr_vector.h"
24	#include "vtr_random.h"
25	#include "lazy_pop_unique_priority_queue.h"
26	
27	// Forward declarations
28	class AtomNetlist;
29	class AttractionInfo;
30	class FlatPlacementInfo;
31	class PreClusterTimingManager;
32	class Prepacker;
33	class t_pack_high_fanout_thresholds;
34	struct t_molecule_stats;
35	struct t_packer_opts;
36	
37	/**
38	 * @brief Stats on the gain of a cluster.
39	 *
40	 * This contains information that is updated whenever a molecule is packed into
41	 * a cluster. This information is used to select candidate molecules to pack
42	 * into the given cluster.
43	 */
44	struct ClusterGainStats {
45	    /// @brief The seed molecule used to create this cluster.
46	    PackMoleculeId seed_molecule_id = PackMoleculeId::INVALID();
47	
48	    /// @brief Has this cluster tried to get candidates by connectivity and
49	    ///        timing yet. This helps ensure that we only do that once per
50	    ///        cluster candidate proposal.
51	    bool has_done_connectivity_and_timing = false;
52	
53	    /// @brief Attraction (inverse of cost) function.
54	    std::unordered_map<AtomBlockId, float> gain;
55	
56	    /// @brief The timing criticality score of this atom.
57	    ///        Determined by the most critical atom net between this atom
58	    ///        and any atom in the current pb.
59	    std::unordered_map<AtomBlockId, float> timing_gain;
60	    /// @brief Weighted sum of connections to attraction function.
61	    std::unordered_map<AtomBlockId, float> connection_gain;
62	    /// @brief How many nets on an atom are already in the pb under
63	    ///        consideration.
64	    std::unordered_map<AtomBlockId, float> sharing_gain;
65	
66	    /// @brief Stores the number of times molecules have failed to be packed
67	    ///        into the cluster.
68	    ///
69	    /// key: molecule id, value: number of times the molecule has failed to be
70	    ///                          packed into the cluster.
71	    std::unordered_map<PackMoleculeId, int> mol_failures;
72	
73	    /// @brief List of nets with the num_pins_of_net_in_pb and gain entries
74	    ///        altered (i.e. have some gain-related connection to the current
75	    ///        cluster).
76	    std::vector<AtomNetId> marked_nets;
77	    /// @brief List of blocks with the num_pins_of_net_in_pb and gain entries altered.
78	    std::vector<AtomBlockId> marked_blocks;
79	
80	    /// @brief If no marked candidate molecules, use this high fanout net to
81	    ///        determine the next candidate atom.
82	    AtomNetId tie_break_high_fanout_net;
83	    /// @brief If no marked candidate molecules and no high fanout nets to
84	    ///        determine next candidate molecule then explore molecules on
85	    ///        transitive fanout.
86	    bool explore_transitive_fanout;
87	    /// @brief Holding transitive fanout candidates key: root block id of the
88	    ///        molecule, value: pointer to the molecule.
89	    // TODO: This should be an unordered map, unless stability is desired.
90	    std::map<AtomBlockId, PackMoleculeId> transitive_fanout_candidates;
91	
92	    /// @brief How many pins of each atom net are contained in the currently open pb?
93	    std::unordered_map<AtomNetId, int> num_pins_of_net_in_pb;
94	
95	    /// @brief The attraction group associated with the cluster. Will be
96	    ///        AttractGroupId::INVALID() if no attraction group is associated
97	    ///        with the cluster.
98	    AttractGroupId attraction_grp_id;
99	
100	    /// @brief The flat placement location of this cluster.
101	    ///
102	    /// This is some function of the positions of the molecules which have been
103	    /// packed into this cluster. How this position is computed is decided by
104	    /// the appack_options passed into the candidate selector class.
105	    ///
106	    /// This is only set and used when APPack is used.
107	    t_flat_pl_loc flat_cluster_position;
108	
109	    /// @brief The sum of the positions of all molecules in this cluster.
110	    ///
111	    /// This sum can be useful for quickly computing the centroid of this
112	    /// cluster. This sum is updated whenever a molecule is successfully added
113	    /// to the cluster.
114	    ///
115	    /// This is only set and used when APPack is used.
116	    t_flat_pl_loc mol_pos_sum;
117	
118	    /// @brief Flag to indicate if this cluster is a memory or not. This is
119	    ///        set when the stats are created based on the primitive pb type
120	    ///        of the seed.
121	    bool is_memory = false;
122	
123	    /// @brief The logical RAM group ID of this cluster's seed atom.
124	    ///        Set to INVALID for non-memory clusters.
125	    LogicalRamGroupId logical_ram_id;
126	
127	    /// @brief The physical RAM group ID of this cluster's seed atom.
128	    ///        Set to INVALID for non-memory clusters.
129	    PhysicalRamGroupId physical_ram_id;
130	
131	    /// @brief List of feasible block and its gain pairs.
132	    ///        The list is maintained in heap structure with the highest gain block
133	    ///        at the front.
134	    LazyPopUniquePriorityQueue<PackMoleculeId, float> feasible_blocks;
135	
136	    /// @brief Indicator for the initial search for feasible blocks.
137	    bool initial_search_for_feasible_blocks;
138	
139	    /// @brief Limit for the number of candidates proposed at each stage.
140	    unsigned candidates_propose_limit;
141	
142	    /// @brief Counter for the number of candidates proposed at each stage.
143	    unsigned num_candidates_proposed;
144	
145	    /// @brief Check if the current stage candidates proposed limit is reached.
146	    bool current_stage_candidates_proposed_limit_reached() {
147	        return num_candidates_proposed >= candidates_propose_limit;
148	    }
149	};
150	
151	/**
152	 * @brief A selector class which will propose good candidate molecules to pack
153	 *        into the given cluster. This is used to grow clusters in a greedy
154	 *        clusterer.
155	 *
156	 * In greedy clustering algorithms, clusters are grown by selecting the
157	 * candidate molecule with the highest gain to pack into it. This class
158	 * calculates and maintains the gains on clusters and their candidates to allow
159	 * it to select which candidate to try next.
160	 *
161	 * Usage:
162	 *
163	 * GreedyCandidateSelector candidate_selector(...);
164	 *
165	 * // ... (Start a new cluster using the cluster legalizer)
166	 *
167	 * // Create an object to hold the gain statistics for the new cluster.
168	 * ClusterGainStats cluster_gain_stats = candidate_selector.create_cluster_gain_stats(...);
169	 *
170	 * // Select a candidate to pack into the cluster using the gain stats.
171	 * PackMoleculeId candidate_mol = candidate_selector.get_next_candidate_for_cluster(cluster_gain_stats, ...);
172	 *
173	 * // ... (Try to pack the candidate into the cluster)
174	 *
175	 * // Update the cluster gain stats based on if the pack was successful or not.
176	 * if (pack succeeded):
177	 *   candidate_selector.update_cluster_gain_stats_candidate_success(cluster_gain_stats, candidate_mol, ...);
178	 * else:
179	 *   candidate_selector.candidate_selector.update_cluster_gain_stats_candidate_failed(cluster_gain_stats, candidate_mol);
180	 *
181	 * // Pick a new candidate and continue
182	 * candidate_mol = candidate_selector.get_next_candidate_for_cluster(cluster_gain_stats, ...);
183	 * ...
184	 *
185	 * // Once the cluster is fully packed, finalize the cluster.
186	 * candidate_selector.update_candidate_selector_finalize_cluster(cluster_gain_stats, ...);
187	 */
188	class GreedyCandidateSelector {
189	  private:
190	    /// @brief How many unrelated candidates can be proposed and not clustered
191	    ///        in a row. So if an unrelated candidate is successfully clustered,
192	    ///        the counter is reset.
193	    static constexpr int max_unrelated_clustering_attempts_ = 1;
194	
195	    /// @brief For high-fanout nets that are ignored, consider a maximum of this
196	    ///        many sinks, must be less than packer_opts.feasible_block_array_size.
197	    static constexpr int AAPACK_MAX_HIGH_FANOUT_EXPLORE = 10;
198	
199	    /// @brief When investigating transitive fanout connections in packing,
200	    ///        consider a maximum of this many molecules, must be less than
201	    ///        packer_opts.feasible_block_array_size.
202	    static constexpr int AAPACK_MAX_TRANSITIVE_EXPLORE = 40;
203	
204	    /// @brief When adding cluster molecule candidates by attraction groups,
205	    ///        only investigate this many candidates. Some attraction groups can
206	    ///        get very large; so this threshold decides when to explore all
207	    ///        atoms in the group, or a randomly selected number of them.
208	    static constexpr int attraction_group_num_atoms_threshold_ = 500;
209	
210	  public:
211	    ~GreedyCandidateSelector();
212	
213	    /**
214	     * @brief Constructor of the Greedy Candidate Selector class. Pre-computes
215	     *        data used by the candidate selector.
216	     *
217	     *  @param atom_netlist
218	     *              The netlist of atoms to cluster.
219	     *  @param prepacker
220	     *              The prepacker used to generate pack-pattern molecules of the
221	     *              atoms in the netlist.
222	     *  @param packer_opts
223	     *              Options passed by the user to configure the packing
224	     *              algorithm. Changes how the candidates are selected.
225	     *  @param allow_unrelated_clustering
226	     *              Enables an algorithm in the selector to look for good
227	     *              candidates which are not necessarily connected to the
228	     *              cluster.
229	     *  @param max_molecule_stats
230	     *              The maximum stats over all molecules. Used for normalizing
231	     *              terms in the gain.
232	     *  @param primitive_candidate_block_types
233	     *              Candidate logical block types which are compatible with the
234	     *              given primitive model.
235	     *  @param high_fanout_thresholds
236	     *              The thresholds for what to consider as a high-fanout net
237	     *              for each logical block type. The clusterer will not consider
238	     *              nets with fanout higher than this to be important.
239	     *  @param is_clock
240	     *              The set of clock nets in the Atom Netlist.
241	     *  @param is_global
242	     *              The set of global nets in the Atom Netlist. These will be
243	     *              routed on special dedicated networks, and hence are less
244	     *              relevant to locality / attraction.
245	     *  @param net_output_feeds_driving_block_input
246	     *              The set of nets whose output feeds the block that drives
247	     *              itself. This may cause double-counting in the gain
248	     *              calculations and needs special handling.
249	     *  @param pre_cluster_timing_manager
250	     *              Timing manager that holds the information on timing of
251	     *              different connections in the circuit. Used for computing
252	     *              the timing gain terms.
253	     *  @param appack_ctx
254	     *              The APPack context which contains options for the flat
255	     *              placement guided packing.
256	     *  @param log_verbosity
257	     *              The verbosity of log messages in the candidate selector.
258	     */
259	    GreedyCandidateSelector(const AtomNetlist& atom_netlist,
260	                            const Prepacker& prepacker,
261	                            const RamMapper& ram_mapper,
262	                            const t_packer_opts& packer_opts,
263	                            bool allow_unrelated_clustering,
264	                            const t_molecule_stats& max_molecule_stats,
265	                            const vtr::vector<LogicalModelId, std::vector<t_logical_block_type_ptr>>& primitive_candidate_block_types,
266	                            const t_pack_high_fanout_thresholds& high_fanout_thresholds,
267	                            const std::unordered_set<AtomNetId>& is_clock,
268	                            const std::unordered_set<AtomNetId>& is_global,
269	                            const std::unordered_set<AtomNetId>& net_output_feeds_driving_block_input,
270	                            const PreClusterTimingManager& pre_cluster_timing_manager,
271	                            const APPackContext& appack_ctx,
272	                            const LogicalModels& models,
273	                            int log_verbosity);
274	
275	    /**
276	     * @brief Create and initialize the gain stats for the cluster with the
277	     *        given cluster_id which was created with the given cluster_seed_mol.
278	     *
279	     * Used when a seed is used to create a new cluster in a greedy clusterer.
280	     *
281	     * The returned ClusterGainStats object is used to store statistics on the
282	     * cluster for selecting the candidate with the highest gain and keep track
283	     * of which molecules have been tried.
284	     *
285	     *  @param cluster_seed_mol
286	     *              The seed molecule which was used to create the cluster.
287	     *  @param cluster_id
288	     *              The legalization cluster ID of the cluster.
289	     *  @param cluster_legalizer
290	     *              The legalizer used to create the cluster.
291	     *  @param attraction_groups
292	     *              Groups of primitives that have extra attraction to each
293	     *              other.
294	     */
295	    ClusterGainStats create_cluster_gain_stats(
296	        PackMoleculeId cluster_seed_mol_id,
297	        LegalizationClusterId cluster_id,
298	        const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
299	        AttractionInfo& attraction_groups);
300	
301	    /**
302	     * @brief Update the cluster gain stats given that the successful_mol was
303	     *        packed successfully into the cluster.
304	     *
305	     * This marks and updates the gain stats for each block in the successfully
306	     * clustered molecule.
307	     *
308	     *  @param cluster_gain_stats
309	     *              The cluster gain stats to update.
310	     *  @param successful_mol
311	     *              The molecule which was successfully packed into the cluster.
312	     *  @param cluster_id
313	     *              The legalization cluster ID of the cluster.
314	     *  @param cluster_legalizer
315	     *              The legalizer used to create the cluster.
316	     *  @param attraction_groups
317	     *              Groups of primitives that have extra attraction to each
318	     *              other.
319	     */
320	    void update_cluster_gain_stats_candidate_success(
321	        ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
322	        PackMoleculeId successful_mol_id,
323	        LegalizationClusterId cluster_id,
324	        const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
325	        AttractionInfo& attraction_groups);
326	
327	    /**
328	     * @brief Update the cluster gain stats given that the failed_mol was not
329	     *        packed successfully into the cluster.
330	     *
331	     * This tracks the failed molecule to help decide future molecules to
332	     * select.
333	     *
334	     *  @param cluster_gain_stats
335	     *              The cluster gain stats to update.
336	     *  @param failed_mol
337	     *              The molecule that failed to pack into the cluster.
338	     */
339	    void update_cluster_gain_stats_candidate_failed(
340	        ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
341	        PackMoleculeId failed_mol_id);
342	
343	    /**
344	     * @brief Given the cluster_gain_stats, select the next candidate molecule
345	     *        to pack into the given cluster.
346	     *
347	     * This uses the gain stats to find the unclustered molecule which will
348	     * likely have the highest gain.
349	     *
350	     *  @param cluster_gain_stats
351	     *              The cluster gain stats maintained for this cluster.
352	     *  @param cluster_id
353	     *              The legalization cluster id for the cluster.
354	     *  @param cluster_legalizer
355	     *              The legalizer used to create the cluster.
356	     *  @param attraction_groups
357	     *              Groups of primitives that have extra attraction to each
358	     *              other.
359	     */
360	    PackMoleculeId get_next_candidate_for_cluster(
361	        ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
362	        LegalizationClusterId cluster_id,
363	        const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
364	        AttractionInfo& attraction_groups);
365	
366	    /**
367	     * @brief Finalize the creation of a cluster.
368	     *
369	     * This should be called after all molecules have been packed into a cluster.
370	     *
371	     * This updates internal lookup tables in the candidate selector. For
372	     * example, what inter-clb nets exist on a cluster are stored by this
373	     * routine to make later transistive gain function calculations more
374	     * efficient.
375	     *
376	     *  @param cluster_gain_stats
377	     *              The cluster gain stats for the cluster to finalize.
378	     *  @param cluster_id
379	     *              The legalization cluster id of the cluster to finalize.
380	     */
381	    void update_candidate_selector_finalize_cluster(
382	        ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
383	        LegalizationClusterId cluster_id);
384	
385	  private:
386	    // ===================================================================== //
387	    //                      Initializing Data Structures
388	    // ===================================================================== //
389	
390	    /**
391	     * @brief Initialize data structures used for unrelated clustering.
392	     *
393	     * This must be called before using the get_unrelated_candidate methods.
394	     *
395	     *  @param max_molecule_stats
396	     *      The maximum molecule statistics over all molecules in the design.
397	     *      This is used to allocate the data-structures used for unrelated
398	     *      clustering.
399	     */
400	    void initialize_unrelated_clustering_data(
401	        const t_molecule_stats& max_molecule_stats,
402	        const LogicalModels& models);
403	
404	    // ===================================================================== //
405	    //                      Cluster Gain Stats Updating
406	    // ===================================================================== //
407	
408	    /**
409	     * @brief Flag used to decide if the gains of affected blocks should be
410	     *        updated when a block is marked.
411	     */
412	    enum class e_gain_update : bool {
413	        GAIN,   // Update the gains of affected blocks.
414	        NO_GAIN // Do not update the gains of affected blocks.
415	    };
416	
417	    /**
418	     * @brief Flag used to indicate if the net is an input or output when
419	     *        updating the connection gain values.
420	     */
421	    enum class e_net_relation_to_clustered_block : bool {
422	        INPUT, // This is an input net.
423	        OUTPUT // This is an output net.
424	    };
425	
426	    /**
427	     * @brief Updates the marked data structures, and, if gain_flag is GAIN, the
428	     *        gain when an atom block is added to a cluster.
429	     */
430	    void mark_and_update_partial_gain(ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
431	                                      AtomNetId net_id,
432	                                      e_gain_update gain_flag,
433	                                      AtomBlockId clustered_blk_id,
434	                                      const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
435	                                      int high_fanout_net_threshold,
436	                                      e_net_relation_to_clustered_block net_relation_to_clustered_block);
437	
438	    /**
439	     * @brief Updates the connection_gain in the cluster_gain_stats.
440	     */
441	    void update_connection_gain_values(ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
442	                                       AtomNetId net_id,
443	                                       AtomBlockId clustered_blk_id,
444	                                       const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
445	                                       e_net_relation_to_clustered_block net_relation_to_clustered_block);
446	
447	    /**
448	     * Updates the timing_gain in the cluster_gain_stats.
449	     */
450	    void update_timing_gain_values(ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
451	                                   AtomNetId net_id,
452	                                   const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
453	                                   e_net_relation_to_clustered_block net_relation_to_clustered_block);
454	
455	    /**
456	     * @brief Updates the total gain array to reflect the desired tradeoff
457	     *        between input sharing (sharing_gain) and path_length minimization
458	     *        (timing_gain) input each time a new molecule is added to the
459	     *        cluster.
460	     */
461	    void update_total_gain(ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
462	                           AttractionInfo& attraction_groups);
463	
464	    // ===================================================================== //
465	    //                      Cluster Candidate Selection
466	    // ===================================================================== //
467	
468	    /**
469	     * @brief Populate the feasible blocks list for the given cluster using
470	     *        net-traversal based candidate search. Candidates are found
471	     *        progressively by connectivity and timing, transitive connections,
472	     *        high-fanout nets, and attraction groups.
473	     */
474	    void add_general_cluster_molecule_candidates(
475	        ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
476	        LegalizationClusterId legalization_cluster_id,
477	        const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
478	        AttractionInfo& attraction_groups);
479	
480	    /**
481	     * @brief Populate the feasible blocks list for a RAM cluster by offering
482	     *        all unclustered atoms from the same physical RAM group as the
483	     *        cluster seed.
484	     *        TODO: Although this function adds all unclustered atoms in the
485	     *        physical RAM group to feasible blocks list, the caller (get_next_candidate_for_cluster)
486	     *        still uses candidate propose limit. We can hoist the RAM clustering
487	     *        path and create their clusters in a similar way to "flat-recon" without
488	     *        any propose limit as we are trying to legalize physical RAM groups here.
489	     */
490	    void add_ram_cluster_molecule_candidates(
491	        ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
492	        LegalizationClusterId legalization_cluster_id,
493	        const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
494	        AttractionInfo& attraction_groups);
495	
496	    /**
497	     * @brief Add molecules with strong connectedness to the current cluster to
498	     *        the list of feasible blocks.
499	     */
500	    void add_cluster_molecule_candidates_by_connectivity_and_timing(
501	        ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
502	        LegalizationClusterId legalization_cluster_id,
503	        const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
504	        AttractionInfo& attraction_groups);
505	
506	    /**
507	     * @brief Score unclustered atoms that are two hops away from current
508	     *        cluster
509	     *
510	     * For example, consider a cluster that has a FF feeding an adder in another
511	     * cluster. Since this FF is feeding an adder that is packed in another
512	     * cluster this function should find other FFs that are feeding other inputs
513	     * of this adder since they are two hops away from the FF packed in this
514	     * cluster
515	     *
516	     * This is used when adding molecule candidates by transistive connectivity.
517	     */
518	    void load_transitive_fanout_candidates(
519	        ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
520	        LegalizationClusterId legalization_cluster_id,
521	        const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer);
522	
523	    /**
524	     * @brief Add molecules based on transitive connections (eg. 2 hops away)
525	     *        with current cluster.
526	     */
527	    void add_cluster_molecule_candidates_by_transitive_connectivity(
528	        ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
529	        LegalizationClusterId legalization_cluster_id,
530	        const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
531	        AttractionInfo& attraction_groups);
532	
533	    /**
534	     * @brief Add molecules based on weak connectedness (connected by high
535	     *        fanout nets) with current cluster.
536	     */
537	    void add_cluster_molecule_candidates_by_highfanout_connectivity(
538	        ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
539	        LegalizationClusterId legalization_cluster_id,
540	        const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
541	        AttractionInfo& attraction_groups);
542	
543	    /**
544	     * @brief If the current cluster being packed has an attraction group
545	     *        associated with it (i.e. there are atoms in it that belong to an
546	     *        attraction group), this routine adds molecules from the associated
547	     *        attraction group to the list of feasible blocks for the cluster.
548	     *
549	     * Attraction groups can be very large, so we only add some randomly
550	     * selected molecules for efficiency if the number of atoms in the group is
551	     * greater than some threshold. Therefore, the molecules added to the
552	     * candidates will vary each time you call this function.
553	     */
554	    void add_cluster_molecule_candidates_by_attraction_group(
555	        ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
556	        LegalizationClusterId legalization_cluster_id,
557	        const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
558	        AttractionInfo& attraction_groups);
559	
560	    /**
561	     * @brief Finds a molecule to propose which is unrelated but may be good to
562	     *        cluster.
563	     */
564	    PackMoleculeId get_unrelated_candidate_for_cluster(
565	        LegalizationClusterId cluster_id,
566	        const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer);
567	
568	    /**
569	     * @brief Finds a molecule to propose which is unrelated to the current
570	     *        cluster but may be good to pack.
571	     *
572	     * This uses flat placement information to choose a good candidate.
573	     *
574	     * This returns an invalid molecule ID if a candidate cannot be found.
575	     */
576	    PackMoleculeId get_unrelated_candidate_for_cluster_appack(
577	        ClusterGainStats& cluster_gain_stats,
578	        LegalizationClusterId cluster_id,
579	        const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer);
580	
581	    // ===================================================================== //
582	    //                      Internal Variables
583	    // ===================================================================== //
584	
585	    /// @brief The atom netlist to cluster over.
586	    const AtomNetlist& atom_netlist_;
587	
588	    /// @brief The prepacker used to pack atoms into molecule pack patterns.
589	    const Prepacker& prepacker_;
590	
591	    /// @brief Used to look up the logical RAM group of an atom for memory cluster filtering.
592	    const RamMapper& ram_mapper_;
593	
594	    /// @brief True if the RAM mapper has at least one logical RAM group.
595	    ///        Used to guard RAM-specific candidate filtering.
596	    bool has_ram_groups_ = false;
597	
598	    /// @brief The packer options used to configure the clusterer.
599	    const t_packer_opts& packer_opts_;
600	
601	    /// @brief Whether unrelated clustering should be performed or not.
602	    const bool allow_unrelated_clustering_;
603	
604	    /// @brief The verbosity of log messages in the candidate selector.
605	    const int log_verbosity_;
606	
607	    /// @brief Pre-computed vector of logical block types that could implement
608	    ///        the given model in the architecture.
609	    const vtr::vector<LogicalModelId, std::vector<t_logical_block_type_ptr>>& primitive_candidate_block_types_;
610	
611	    /// @brief The high-fanout thresholds per logical block type. Used to ignore
612	    ///        certain nets when calculating the gain for the next candidate
613	    ///        molecule to cluster.
614	    // TODO: This should really be a map from the logical block type to the
615	    //       threshold.
616	    const t_pack_high_fanout_thresholds& high_fanout_thresholds_;
617	
618	    /// @brief A set of atom nets which are considered as clocks.
619	    const std::unordered_set<AtomNetId>& is_clock_;
620	
621	    /// @brief A set of atom nets which are considered as global nets.
622	    const std::unordered_set<AtomNetId>& is_global_;
623	
624	    /// @brief A set of atom nets which have outputs that feed the block that
625	    ///        drive them.
626	    const std::unordered_set<AtomNetId>& net_output_feeds_driving_block_input_;
627	
628	    /// @brief The pre-clustering timing manager which holds the timing information
629	    ///        of the primitive netlist.
630	    const PreClusterTimingManager& pre_cluster_timing_manager_;
631	
632	    /// @brief Inter-block nets within a finalized cluster. Used for finding
633	    ///        transitive candidates.
634	    vtr::vector<LegalizationClusterId, std::vector<AtomNetId>> clb_inter_blk_nets_;
635	
636	    /// @brief Data pre-computed to help select unrelated molecules. This is a
637	    ///        list of list of molecules sorted by their gain, where the first
638	    ///        dimension is the number of external outputs of the molecule.
639	    std::vector<std::vector<PackMoleculeId>> unrelated_clustering_data_;
640	
641	    /// @brief Data pre-computed to help select unrelated molecules when APPack
642	    ///        is being used. This is the same data as unrelated_clustering_data_,
643	    ///        but it is spatially distributed over the device.
644	    /// For each grid location on the device (layer, x, y), this provides a list of
645	    /// molecules sorted by their gain, where the first dimension is the number
646	    /// of external outputs of the molecule.
647	    /// When APPack is not used, this will be uninitialized.
648	    ///     [0..flat_grid_num_layers][0..flat_grid_width][0..flat_grid_height][0..max_num_used_ext_pins]
649	    /// Here, flat_grid width/height is the maximum x and y positions given in
650	    /// the flat placement.
651	    vtr::NdMatrix<std::vector<std::vector<PackMoleculeId>>, 3> appack_unrelated_clustering_data_;
652	
653	    /// @brief The APPack state which contains the options used to configure
654	    ///        APPack and the flat placement.
655	    const APPackContext& appack_ctx_;
656	
657	    /// @brief A count on the number of unrelated clustering attempts which
658	    ///        have been performed.
659	    int num_unrelated_clustering_attempts_ = 0;
660	
661	    /// @brief Random number generator used by the clusterer. Currently this
662	    ///        is used only when selecting atoms from attraction groups, but
663	    ///        could be used for other purposes in the future.
664	    vtr::RngContainer rng_;
665	};

1	/**
2	 * @file
3	 * @author  Vaughn Betz (first revision - VPack),
4	 *          Alexander Marquardt (second revision - T-VPack),
5	 *          Jason Luu (third revision - AAPack),
6	 *          Alex Singer (fourth revision - APPack)
7	 * @date    June 8, 2011
8	 * @brief   Main clustering algorithm
9	 *
10	 * The clusterer uses several key data structures:
11	 *
12	 *      t_pb_type (and related types):
13	 *          Represent the architecture as described in the architecture file.
14	 *
15	 *      t_pb_graph_node (and related types):
16	 *          Represents a flattened version of the architecture with t_pb_types
17	 *          expanded (according to num_pb) into unique t_pb_graph_node instances,
18	 *          and the routing connectivity converted to a graph of t_pb_graph_pin (nodes)
19	 *          and t_pb_graph_edge.
20	 *
21	 *      t_pb:
22	 *          Represents a clustered instance of a t_pb_graph_node containing netlist primitives
23	 *
24	 *  t_pb_type and t_pb_graph_node (and related types) describe the targeted FPGA architecture, while t_pb represents
25	 *  the actual clustering of the user netlist.
26	 *
27	 *  For example:
28	 *      Consider an architecture where CLBs contain 4 BLEs, and each BLE is a LUT + FF pair.
29	 *      We wish to map a netlist of 400 LUTs and 400 FFs.
30	 *
31	 *      A BLE corresponds to one t_pb_type (which has num_pb = 4).
32	 *
33	 *      Each of the 4 BLE positions corresponds to a t_pb_graph_node (each of which references the BLE t_pb_type).
34	 *
35	 *      The output of clustering is 400 t_pb of type BLE which represent the clustered user netlist.
36	 *      Each of the 400 t_pb will reference one of the 4 BLE-type t_pb_graph_nodes.
37	 */
38	
39	#include "greedy_clusterer.h"
40	#include <cstdio>
41	#include <map>
42	#include <string>
43	#include <vector>
44	#include "appack_context.h"
45	#include "setup_grid.h"
46	#include "atom_netlist.h"
47	#include "attraction_groups.h"
48	#include "cluster_legalizer.h"
49	#include "cluster_util.h"
50	#include "greedy_candidate_selector.h"
51	#include "greedy_seed_selector.h"
52	#include "logic_types.h"
53	#include "physical_types.h"
54	#include "prepack.h"
55	#include "logical_ram_infer.h"
56	#include "vpr_context.h"
57	#include "vtr_math.h"
58	
59	namespace {
60	
61	/**
62	 * @brief Struct to hold statistics on the progress of clustering.
63	 */
64	struct t_cluster_progress_stats {
65	    // The total number of molecules in the design.
66	    int num_molecules = 0;
67	    // The number of molecules which have been clustered.
68	    int num_molecules_processed = 0;
69	    // The number of molecules clustered since the last time the status was
70	    // logged.
71	    int mols_since_last_print = 0;
72	};
73	
74	} // namespace
75	
76	GreedyClusterer::GreedyClusterer(const t_packer_opts& packer_opts,
77	                                 const t_analysis_opts& analysis_opts,
78	                                 const AtomNetlist& atom_netlist,
79	                                 const t_arch& arch,
80	                                 const t_pack_high_fanout_thresholds& high_fanout_thresholds,
81	                                 const std::unordered_set<AtomNetId>& is_clock,
82	                                 const std::unordered_set<AtomNetId>& is_global,
83	                                 const PreClusterTimingManager& pre_cluster_timing_manager,
84	                                 const APPackContext& appack_ctx,
85	                                 const t_vpr_setup& vpr_setup)
86	    : packer_opts_(packer_opts)
87	    , vpr_setup_(vpr_setup)
88	    , analysis_opts_(analysis_opts)
89	    , atom_netlist_(atom_netlist)
90	    , arch_(arch)
91	    , high_fanout_thresholds_(high_fanout_thresholds)
92	    , is_clock_(is_clock)
93	    , is_global_(is_global)
94	    , pre_cluster_timing_manager_(pre_cluster_timing_manager)
95	    , appack_ctx_(appack_ctx)
96	    , primitive_candidate_block_types_(identify_primitive_candidate_block_types())
97	    , log_verbosity_(packer_opts.pack_verbosity)
98	    , net_output_feeds_driving_block_input_(identify_net_output_feeds_driving_block_input(atom_netlist)) {
99	}
100	
101	std::map<t_logical_block_type_ptr, size_t>
102	GreedyClusterer::do_clustering(ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
103	                               const Prepacker& prepacker,
104	                               const RamMapper& ram_mapper,
105	                               bool allow_unrelated_clustering,
106	                               bool balance_block_type_utilization,
107	                               AttractionInfo& attraction_groups,
108	                               DeviceContext& mutable_device_ctx) {
109	    // This routine returns a map that details the number of used block type
110	    // instances.
111	    std::map<t_logical_block_type_ptr, size_t> num_used_type_instances;
112	
113	    /****************************************************************
114	     * Initialization
115	     *****************************************************************/
116	
117	    // The clustering stats holds information used for logging the progress
118	    // of the clustering to the user.
119	    t_cluster_progress_stats clustering_stats;
120	    clustering_stats.num_molecules = prepacker.molecules().size();
121	
122	    // Check whether the RAM mapper has any groups to guard RAM-specific logic.
123	    has_ram_groups_ = ram_mapper.num_groups() > 0;
124	
125	    // Calculate the max molecule stats, which is used for gain calculation.
126	    const t_molecule_stats max_molecule_stats = prepacker.calc_max_molecule_stats(atom_netlist_, arch_.models);
127	
128	    // Create the greedy candidate selector. This will be used to select
129	    // candidate molecules to add to the clusters.
130	    GreedyCandidateSelector candidate_selector(atom_netlist_,
131	                                               prepacker,
132	                                               ram_mapper,
133	                                               packer_opts_,
134	                                               allow_unrelated_clustering,
135	                                               max_molecule_stats,
136	                                               primitive_candidate_block_types_,
137	                                               high_fanout_thresholds_,
138	                                               is_clock_,
139	                                               is_global_,
140	                                               net_output_feeds_driving_block_input_,
141	                                               pre_cluster_timing_manager_,
142	                                               appack_ctx_,
143	                                               arch_.models,
144	                                               log_verbosity_);
145	
146	    // Create the greedy seed selector.
147	    GreedySeedSelector seed_selector(atom_netlist_,
148	                                     prepacker,
149	                                     packer_opts_.cluster_seed_type,
150	                                     max_molecule_stats,
151	                                     arch_.models,
152	                                     pre_cluster_timing_manager_,
153	                                     ram_mapper);
154	
155	    // Pick the first seed molecule.
156	    PackMoleculeId seed_mol_id = seed_selector.get_next_seed(cluster_legalizer);
157	
158	    /****************************************************************
159	     * Clustering
160	     *****************************************************************/
161	
162	    print_pack_status_header();
163	
164	    // Continue clustering as long as a valid seed is returned from the seed
165	    // selector.
166	    while (seed_mol_id.is_valid()) {
167	        // Check to ensure that this molecule is unclustered.
168	        VTR_ASSERT(!cluster_legalizer.is_mol_clustered(seed_mol_id));
169	
170	        // The basic algorithm:
171	        // 1) Try to put all the molecules in that you can without doing the
172	        //    full intra-lb route. Then do full legalization at the end.
173	        // 2) If the legalization at the end fails, try again, but this time
174	        //    do full legalization for each molecule added to the cluster.
175	
176	        // Try to grow a cluster from the seed molecule without doing intra-lb
177	        // route for each molecule (i.e. just use faster but not fully
178	        // conservative legality checks).
179	        LegalizationClusterId new_cluster_id = try_grow_cluster(seed_mol_id,
180	                                                                candidate_selector,
181	                                                                ClusterLegalizationStrategy::SKIP_INTRA_LB_ROUTE,
182	                                                                cluster_legalizer,
183	                                                                prepacker,
184	                                                                ram_mapper,
185	                                                                balance_block_type_utilization,
186	                                                                attraction_groups,
187	                                                                num_used_type_instances,
188	                                                                mutable_device_ctx);
189	
190	        if (!new_cluster_id.is_valid()) {
191	            // If the previous strategy failed, try to grow the cluster again,
192	            // but this time perform full legalization for each molecule added
193	            // to the cluster.
194	            new_cluster_id = try_grow_cluster(seed_mol_id,
195	                                              candidate_selector,
196	                                              ClusterLegalizationStrategy::FULL,
197	                                              cluster_legalizer,
198	                                              prepacker,
199	                                              ram_mapper,
200	                                              balance_block_type_utilization,
201	                                              attraction_groups,
202	                                              num_used_type_instances,
203	                                              mutable_device_ctx);
204	        }
205	
206	        // Ensure that the seed was packed successfully.
207	        VTR_ASSERT(new_cluster_id.is_valid());
208	        VTR_ASSERT(cluster_legalizer.is_mol_clustered(seed_mol_id));
209	
210	        // Update the clustering progress stats.
211	        size_t num_molecules_in_cluster = cluster_legalizer.get_num_molecules_in_cluster(new_cluster_id);
212	        clustering_stats.num_molecules_processed += num_molecules_in_cluster;
213	        clustering_stats.mols_since_last_print += num_molecules_in_cluster;
214	
215	        // Print the current progress of the packing after a cluster has been
216	        // successfully created.
217	        print_pack_status(clustering_stats.num_molecules,
218	                          clustering_stats.num_molecules_processed,
219	                          clustering_stats.mols_since_last_print,
220	                          mutable_device_ctx.grid.width(),
221	                          mutable_device_ctx.grid.height(),
222	                          attraction_groups,
223	                          cluster_legalizer);
224	
225	        // Pick new seed.
226	        seed_mol_id = seed_selector.get_next_seed(cluster_legalizer);
227	    }
228	
229	    // If this architecture has LE physical block, report its usage.
230	    report_le_physical_block_usage(cluster_legalizer);
231	
232	    return num_used_type_instances;
233	}
234	
235	LegalizationClusterId GreedyClusterer::try_grow_cluster(PackMoleculeId seed_mol_id,
236	                                                        GreedyCandidateSelector& candidate_selector,
237	                                                        ClusterLegalizationStrategy strategy,
238	                                                        ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
239	                                                        const Prepacker& prepacker,
240	                                                        const RamMapper& ram_mapper,
241	                                                        bool balance_block_type_utilization,
242	                                                        AttractionInfo& attraction_groups,
243	                                                        std::map<t_logical_block_type_ptr, size_t>& num_used_type_instances,
244	                                                        DeviceContext& mutable_device_ctx) {
245	
246	    // Check to ensure that this molecule is unclustered.
247	    VTR_ASSERT(!cluster_legalizer.is_mol_clustered(seed_mol_id));
248	
249	    // Set the legalization strategy of the cluster legalizer.
250	    cluster_legalizer.set_legalization_strategy(strategy);
251	
252	    // Use the seed to start a new cluster.
253	    LegalizationClusterId legalization_cluster_id = start_new_cluster(seed_mol_id,
254	                                                                      cluster_legalizer,
255	                                                                      prepacker,
256	                                                                      ram_mapper,
257	                                                                      balance_block_type_utilization,
258	                                                                      num_used_type_instances,
259	                                                                      mutable_device_ctx);
260	
261	    // Create the cluster gain stats. This updates the gains in the candidate
262	    // selector due to a new molecule being clustered.
263	    ClusterGainStats cluster_gain_stats = candidate_selector.create_cluster_gain_stats(seed_mol_id,
264	                                                                                       legalization_cluster_id,
265	                                                                                       cluster_legalizer,
266	                                                                                       attraction_groups);
267	
268	    // Select the first candidate molecule to try to add to this cluster.
269	    PackMoleculeId candidate_mol_id = candidate_selector.get_next_candidate_for_cluster(
270	        cluster_gain_stats,
271	        legalization_cluster_id,
272	        cluster_legalizer,
273	        attraction_groups);
274	
275	    /*
276	     * When attraction groups are created, the purpose is to pack more densely by adding more molecules
277	     * from the cluster's attraction group to the cluster. In a normal flow, (when attraction groups are
278	     * not on), the cluster keeps being packed until the get_molecule routines return either a repeated
279	     * molecule or a nullptr. When attraction groups are on, we want to keep exploring molecules for the
280	     * cluster until a nullptr is returned. So, the number of repeated molecules allowed is increased to a
281	     * large value.
282	     */
283	    int max_num_repeated_molecules = 1;
284	    if (attraction_groups.num_attraction_groups() > 0)
285	        max_num_repeated_molecules = attraction_groups_max_repeated_molecules_;
286	
287	    // Continuously try to cluster candidate molecules into the cluster
288	    // until one of the following occurs:
289	    //  1) No candidate molecule is proposed.
290	    //  2) The same candidate was proposed multiple times.
291	    int num_repeated_molecules = 0;
292	    while (candidate_mol_id.is_valid() && num_repeated_molecules < max_num_repeated_molecules) {
293	        // Try to cluster the candidate molecule into the cluster.
294	        bool success = try_add_candidate_mol_to_cluster(candidate_mol_id,
295	                                                        legalization_cluster_id,
296	                                                        cluster_legalizer,
297	                                                        prepacker);
298	
299	        // If the candidate molecule was clustered successfully, update
300	        // the cluster stats.
301	        if (success) {
302	            // If the last candidate was clustered successfully, update the
303	            // gains in the candidate selector.
304	            candidate_selector.update_cluster_gain_stats_candidate_success(cluster_gain_stats,
305	                                                                           candidate_mol_id,
306	                                                                           legalization_cluster_id,
307	                                                                           cluster_legalizer,
308	                                                                           attraction_groups);
309	        } else {
310	            // If the last candidate was not clustered successfully, update the
311	            // gains in the candidate selector accordingly.
312	            candidate_selector.update_cluster_gain_stats_candidate_failed(cluster_gain_stats,
313	                                                                          candidate_mol_id);
314	        }
315	
316	        // Get the next candidate molecule.
317	        PackMoleculeId prev_candidate_mol_id = candidate_mol_id;
318	        candidate_mol_id = candidate_selector.get_next_candidate_for_cluster(
319	            cluster_gain_stats,
320	            legalization_cluster_id,
321	            cluster_legalizer,
322	            attraction_groups);
323	
324	        // If the next candidate molecule is the same as the previous
325	        // candidate molecule, increment the number of repeated
326	        // molecules counter.
327	        if (candidate_mol_id == prev_candidate_mol_id)
328	            num_repeated_molecules++;
329	    }
330	
331	    // Ensure that the cluster has a legal final routing.
332	    //
333	    // If strategy == SKIP_INTRA_LB_ROUTE, then this is the first and only
334	    // legality check.
335	    //
336	    // If strategy == FULL, then it is possible to reach this step without a
337	    // routing solution if a cluster has been packed the same way before, due
338	    // to skipping routing on clusters that are known to be legal from the
339	    // PackingSignatureTree. In this case, routing must be run at least once on
340	    // the final cluster for later stages to use.
341	    if (!cluster_legalizer.ensure_legal_final_routing(legalization_cluster_id)) {
342	        // If the cluster is not legal, undo the cluster.
343	        // Update the used type instances.
344	        num_used_type_instances[cluster_legalizer.get_cluster_type(legalization_cluster_id)]--;
345	        // Destroy the illegal cluster.
346	        cluster_legalizer.destroy_cluster(legalization_cluster_id);
347	        cluster_legalizer.compress();
348	        // Cluster failed to grow.
349	        return LegalizationClusterId();
350	    }
351	
352	    // A legal cluster must have been created by this point.
353	    VTR_ASSERT(legalization_cluster_id.is_valid());
354	
355	    // After the cluster has been fully created, update internal structures
356	    // to improve the gain calculation.
357	    candidate_selector.update_candidate_selector_finalize_cluster(cluster_gain_stats,
358	                                                                  legalization_cluster_id);
359	
360	    // Since the cluster will no longer be added to beyond this point,
361	    // clean the cluster of any data not strictly necessary for
362	    // creating the clustered netlist.
363	    cluster_legalizer.clean_cluster(legalization_cluster_id);
364	
365	    // Cluster has been grown successfully.
366	    return legalization_cluster_id;
367	}

369	/**
370	 * @brief If the atom is a memory primitive with a pre-assigned RAM type, moves
371	 *        that type to the front of candidate_types using a stable partition so
372	 *        the packer tries it first. Has no effect for non-memory atoms or groups
373	 *        without a pre-assigned type.
374	 *
375	 * @param root_atom        The seed atom being clustered.
376	 * @param prepacker        Used to look up the expected primitive for root_atom.
377	 * @param ram_mapper       Used to look up the logical RAM group and pre-assigned type.
378	 * @param atom_netlist     Used to look up the atom name for warning messages.
379	 * @param candidate_types  Block types to reorder in place.
380	 */
381	static void prioritize_pre_assigned_ram_type(AtomBlockId root_atom,
382	                                             const Prepacker& prepacker,
383	                                             const RamMapper& ram_mapper,
384	                                             const AtomNetlist& atom_netlist,
385	                                             std::vector<t_logical_block_type_ptr>& candidate_types) {
386	    const t_pb_graph_node* prim = prepacker.get_expected_lowest_cost_pb_gnode(root_atom);
387	    if (!prim->pb_type->is_primitive() || prim->pb_type->class_type != MEMORY_CLASS)
388	        return;
389	
390	    const LogicalRamGroupId group_id = ram_mapper.group_id_of(root_atom);
391	    VTR_ASSERT_MSG(group_id.is_valid(), "root_atom of memory class should be mapped to a LogicalRamGroup");
392	    const LogicalRamGroup& ram_group = ram_mapper.group(group_id);
393	
394	    auto it = std::find(ram_group.atoms.begin(), ram_group.atoms.end(), root_atom);
395	    VTR_ASSERT_MSG(it != ram_group.atoms.end(), "Could not find root atom in the retrieved logical ram atoms");
396	
397	    if (ram_group.pre_assigned_type) {
398	        t_logical_block_type_ptr pre_assigned_type = ram_group.pre_assigned_type;
399	        std::stable_partition(candidate_types.begin(), candidate_types.end(),
400	                              [&](t_logical_block_type_ptr p) { return p == pre_assigned_type; });
401	    } else {
402	        VTR_LOG_WARN("No pre-assigned type found for logical RAM group of atom %s\n",
403	                     atom_netlist.block_name(root_atom).c_str());
404	    }
405	}
406	
407	LegalizationClusterId GreedyClusterer::start_new_cluster(
408	    PackMoleculeId seed_mol_id,
409	    ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
410	    const Prepacker& prepacker,
411	    const RamMapper& ram_mapper,
412	    bool balance_block_type_utilization,
413	    std::map<t_logical_block_type_ptr, size_t>& num_used_type_instances,
414	    DeviceContext& mutable_device_ctx) {
415	
416	    VTR_ASSERT(seed_mol_id.is_valid());
417	    const t_pack_molecule& seed_mol = prepacker.get_molecule(seed_mol_id);
418	
419	    /* Allocate a dummy initial cluster and load a atom block as a seed and check if it is legal */
420	    AtomBlockId root_atom = seed_mol.atom_block_ids[seed_mol.root];
421	    const std::string& root_atom_name = atom_netlist_.block_name(root_atom);
422	    LogicalModelId root_model_id = atom_netlist_.block_model(root_atom);
423	    VTR_ASSERT(root_model_id.is_valid());
424	    VTR_ASSERT(!primitive_candidate_block_types_[root_model_id].empty());
425	    std::vector<t_logical_block_type_ptr> candidate_types = primitive_candidate_block_types_[root_model_id];
426	
427	    if (balance_block_type_utilization) {
428	        //We sort the candidate types in ascending order by their current utilization.
429	        //This means that the packer will prefer to use types with lower utilization.
430	        //This is a naive approach to try balancing utilization when multiple types can
431	        //support the same primitive(s).
432	        std::stable_sort(candidate_types.begin(), candidate_types.end(),
433	                         [&](t_logical_block_type_ptr lhs, t_logical_block_type_ptr rhs) {
434	                             int lhs_num_instances = 0;
435	                             int rhs_num_instances = 0;
436	                             // Count number of instances for each type
437	                             for (auto type : lhs->equivalent_tiles)
438	                                 lhs_num_instances += mutable_device_ctx.grid.num_instances(type, -1);
439	                             for (auto type : rhs->equivalent_tiles)
440	                                 rhs_num_instances += mutable_device_ctx.grid.num_instances(type, -1);
441	
442	                             float lhs_util = vtr::safe_ratio<float>(num_used_type_instances[lhs], lhs_num_instances);
443	                             float rhs_util = vtr::safe_ratio<float>(num_used_type_instances[rhs], rhs_num_instances);
444	                             //Lower util first
445	                             return lhs_util < rhs_util;
446	                         });
447	    }
448	
449	    if (has_ram_groups_)
450	        prioritize_pre_assigned_ram_type(root_atom, prepacker, ram_mapper, atom_netlist_, candidate_types);
451	
452	    if (log_verbosity_ > 2) {
453	        VTR_LOG("\tSeed: '%s' (%s)", root_atom_name.c_str(), arch_.models.get_model(root_model_id).name.c_str());
454	        VTR_LOGV(seed_mol.pack_pattern, " molecule_type %s molecule_size %zu",
455	                 seed_mol.pack_pattern->name, seed_mol.atom_block_ids.size());
456	        VTR_LOG("\n");
457	    }
458	
459	    //Try packing into each candidate type
460	    bool success = false;
461	    t_logical_block_type_ptr block_type;
462	    LegalizationClusterId new_cluster_id;
463	    for (auto type : candidate_types) {
464	        //Try packing into each mode
465	        e_block_pack_status pack_result = e_block_pack_status::BLK_STATUS_UNDEFINED;
466	        for (int j = 0; j < type->pb_graph_head->pb_type->num_modes && !success; j++) {
467	            std::tie(pack_result, new_cluster_id) = cluster_legalizer.start_new_cluster(seed_mol_id, type, j);
468	            success = (pack_result == e_block_pack_status::BLK_PASSED);
469	        }
470	
471	        if (success) {
472	            VTR_LOGV(log_verbosity_ > 2, "\tPASSED_SEED: Block Type %s\n", type->name.c_str());
473	            // If clustering succeeds return the new_cluster_id and type.
474	            block_type = type;
475	            break;
476	        } else {
477	            VTR_LOGV(log_verbosity_ > 2, "\tFAILED_SEED: Block Type %s\n", type->name.c_str());
478	        }
479	    }
480	
481	    if (!success) {
482	        //Explored all candidates
483	        if (seed_mol.type == e_pack_pattern_molecule_type::MOLECULE_FORCED_PACK) {
484	            VPR_FATAL_ERROR(VPR_ERROR_PACK,
485	                            "Can not find any logic block that can implement molecule.\n"
486	                            "\tPattern %s %s\n",
487	                            seed_mol.pack_pattern->name,
488	                            root_atom_name.c_str());
489	        } else {
490	            VPR_FATAL_ERROR(VPR_ERROR_PACK,
491	                            "Can not find any logic block that can implement molecule.\n"
492	                            "\tAtom %s (%s)\n",
493	                            root_atom_name.c_str(), arch_.models.model_name(root_model_id).c_str());
494	        }
495	    }
496	
497	    VTR_ASSERT(success);
498	    VTR_ASSERT(new_cluster_id.is_valid());
499	
500	    VTR_LOGV(log_verbosity_ > 2,
501	             "Complex block %zu: '%s' (%s) ", size_t(new_cluster_id),
502	             cluster_legalizer.get_cluster_name(new_cluster_id).c_str(),
503	             cluster_legalizer.get_cluster_type(new_cluster_id)->name.c_str());
504	    VTR_LOGV(log_verbosity_ > 2, ".");
505	    //Progress dot for seed-block
506	    fflush(stdout);
507	
508	    // TODO: Below may make more sense in its own method.
509	
510	    // Successfully created cluster
511	    num_used_type_instances[block_type]++;
512	
513	    /* Expand FPGA size if needed */
514	    // Check used type instances against the possible equivalent physical locations
515	    unsigned int num_instances = 0;
516	    for (auto equivalent_tile : block_type->equivalent_tiles) {
517	        num_instances += mutable_device_ctx.grid.num_instances(equivalent_tile, -1);
518	    }
519	
520	    if (!has_fixed_device_size(vpr_setup_)
521	        && num_used_type_instances[block_type] > num_instances) {
522	        mutable_device_ctx.grid = create_device_grid(packer_opts_.device_layout,
523	                                                     arch_.grid_layouts,
524	                                                     num_used_type_instances,
525	                                                     packer_opts_.target_device_utilization,
526	                                                     vpr_setup_.device_width);
527	    }
528	
529	    return new_cluster_id;
530	}

532	bool GreedyClusterer::try_add_candidate_mol_to_cluster(PackMoleculeId candidate_mol_id,
533	                                                       LegalizationClusterId legalization_cluster_id,
534	                                                       ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer,
535	                                                       const Prepacker& prepacker) {
536	    VTR_ASSERT(candidate_mol_id.is_valid());
537	    VTR_ASSERT(!cluster_legalizer.is_mol_clustered(candidate_mol_id));
538	    VTR_ASSERT(legalization_cluster_id.is_valid());
539	
540	    e_block_pack_status pack_status = cluster_legalizer.add_mol_to_cluster(candidate_mol_id,
541	                                                                           legalization_cluster_id);
542	
543	    // Print helpful debugging log messages.
544	    if (log_verbosity_ > 2) {
545	        switch (pack_status) {
546	            case e_block_pack_status::BLK_PASSED:
547	                VTR_LOG("\tPassed: ");
548	                break;
549	            case e_block_pack_status::BLK_FAILED_ROUTE:
550	                VTR_LOG("\tNO_ROUTE: ");
551	                break;
552	            case e_block_pack_status::BLK_FAILED_FLOORPLANNING:
553	                VTR_LOG("\tFAILED_FLOORPLANNING_CONSTRAINTS_CHECK: ");
554	                break;
555	            case e_block_pack_status::BLK_FAILED_FEASIBLE:
556	                VTR_LOG("\tFAILED_FEASIBILITY_CHECK: ");
557	                break;
558	            case e_block_pack_status::BLK_FAILED_NOC_GROUP:
559	                VTR_LOG("\tFAILED_NOC_GROUP_CHECK: ");
560	                break;
561	            default:
562	                VPR_FATAL_ERROR(VPR_ERROR_PACK, "Unknown pack status thrown.");
563	                break;
564	        }
565	        // Get the block name and model name
566	        const t_pack_molecule& candidate_mol = prepacker.get_molecule(candidate_mol_id);
567	        AtomBlockId blk_id = candidate_mol.atom_block_ids[candidate_mol.root];
568	        VTR_ASSERT(blk_id.is_valid());
569	        std::string blk_name = atom_netlist_.block_name(blk_id);
570	        LogicalModelId blk_model_id = atom_netlist_.block_model(blk_id);
571	        std::string blk_model_name = arch_.models.model_name(blk_model_id);
572	        VTR_LOG("'%s' (%s)", blk_name.c_str(), blk_model_name.c_str());
573	        VTR_LOGV(candidate_mol.pack_pattern, " molecule %s molecule_size %zu",
574	                 candidate_mol.pack_pattern->name,
575	                 candidate_mol.atom_block_ids.size());
576	        VTR_LOG("\n");
577	        fflush(stdout);
578	    }
579	
580	    return pack_status == e_block_pack_status::BLK_PASSED;
581	}
582	
583	void GreedyClusterer::report_le_physical_block_usage(const ClusterLegalizer& cluster_legalizer) {
584	    // find the cluster type that has lut primitives
585	    auto logic_block_type = identify_logic_block_type(primitive_candidate_block_types_);
586	    // find a LE pb_type within the found logic_block_type
587	    auto le_pb_type = identify_le_block_type(logic_block_type);
588	
589	    // If this architecture does not have an LE physical block, cannot report
590	    // its usage.
591	    if (le_pb_type == nullptr)
592	        return;
593	
594	    // Track the number of Logic Elements (LEs) used. This is populated only for
595	    // architectures which has LEs. The architecture is assumed to have LEs iff
596	    // it has a logic block that contains LUT primitives and is the first
597	    // pb_block to have more than one instance from the top of the hierarchy
598	    // (All parent pb_block have one instance only and one mode only).
599	
600	    // The number of LEs that are used for logic (LUTs/adders) only.
601	    int num_logic_le = 0;
602	    // The number of LEs that are used for registers only.
603	    int num_reg_le = 0;
604	    // The number of LEs that are used for both logic (LUTs/adders) and registers.
605	    int num_logic_and_reg_le = 0;
606	
607	    for (LegalizationClusterId cluster_id : cluster_legalizer.clusters()) {
608	        // Update the data structure holding the LE counts
609	        update_le_count(cluster_legalizer.get_cluster_pb(cluster_id),
610	                        logic_block_type,
611	                        le_pb_type,
612	                        num_logic_le,
613	                        num_reg_le,
614	                        num_logic_and_reg_le);
615	    }
616	
617	    // if this architecture has LE physical block, report its usage
618	    if (le_pb_type) {
619	        print_le_count(num_logic_le, num_reg_le, num_logic_and_reg_le, le_pb_type);
620	    }
621	}

input.content:
# 贪心聚簇器 GreedyClusterer

## 1. 本讲目标

上一讲（u4-l2）我们解决了「聚簇器面对的分子从哪里来」：`Prepacker` 把架构里的 pack pattern（如进位链）和原子网表组合成一个个 `t_pack_molecule`。本讲回答下一个问题：**给定一堆分子，如何把它们装进一个个合法的逻辑块簇里？**

VPR 的答案是一个「贪心」算法：一次只造一个簇，从挑一个种子分子开始，然后不断把「增益最高」的候选分子吸进来，直到塞不下或没有更好的候选为止。本讲学完后，你应当能够：

1. 说清 `GreedyClusterer` 一次聚簇迭代的完整流程：选种子 → 长簇 → 合法化。
2. 区分 `GreedySeedSelector`（决定「从谁开始」）与 `GreedyCandidateSelector`（决定「下一个吸谁进来」）的职责，并写出二者的协作顺序。
3. 理解「增益（gain）」是如何由共享、连接、时序、吸引力组等多项加权而成的，以及 APPack / 吸引力组如何提升聚类质量。

## 2. 前置知识

- **分子（molecule）**：u4-l2 引入的概念。聚簇器不直接搬原子，而是搬分子——分子是单个原子或一组被 pack pattern 绑定的原子（如一段进位链），用一个 `PackMoleculeId` 标识。
- **簇（cluster / legalization cluster）**：一个正在被填充的逻辑块实例，用 `LegalizationClusterId` 标识。一个簇最终会变成 `ClusteredNetlist` 里的一个 CLB（见 u3-l3）。
- **合法化（legalization）**：把分子塞进簇不仅要看「容量够不够」，还要看「簇内能不能布线」——即输入能否经由簇内 PB 层次连到分子的输入引脚、分子输出能否连到簇输出。这一步由 `ClusterLegalizer` 完成（下一讲 u4-l4 详讲）。
- **增益（gain）**：一个 0 分制的「把这个分子吸进当前簇有多划算」的打分。增益越高，越优先被吸。
- **PB 图（`t_pb_graph_node`）**：架构 `t_pb_type` 类型树按 `num_pb` 展开成的实例层模板，是簇内布线合法化的依据（见 u4-l1）。
- **`e_packer_state` 状态机**：`try_pack` 用「先紧后松」的多轮策略反复调用 `do_clustering`，装不下就逐步放宽约束（无关聚类、吸引力组等）。本讲聚焦单轮 `do_clustering` 内部。

## 3. 本讲源码地图

| 文件 | 作用 |
| --- | --- |
| [vpr/src/pack/greedy_clusterer.h](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_clusterer.h) | `GreedyClusterer` 类声明，定义聚簇主循环 `do_clustering`、长簇 `try_grow_cluster`、开簇 `start_new_cluster`。 |
| [vpr/src/pack/greedy_clusterer.cpp](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_clusterer.cpp) | 上述方法的实现，是本讲的主干。 |
| [vpr/src/pack/greedy_seed_selector.h](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_seed_selector.h) / [.cpp](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_seed_selector.cpp) | `GreedySeedSelector`：预计算每个分子的「种子增益」并排序，逐个提出好种子。 |
| [vpr/src/pack/greedy_candidate_selector.h](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_candidate_selector.h) / [.cpp](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_candidate_selector.cpp) | `GreedyCandidateSelector`：维护簇的增益统计 `ClusterGainStats`，提出下一个该吸进来的候选分子。 |
| [vpr/src/pack/attraction_groups.h](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/attraction_groups.h) | `AttractionInfo`：吸引力组，把「应被紧密打包在一起」的原子分组并给它们额外增益。 |
| [vpr/src/pack/pack.cpp](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/pack.cpp) | `try_pack` 入口，构造并反复调用 `GreedyClusterer::do_clustering`。 |
| [vpr/src/pack/cluster_legalizer.h](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/cluster_legalizer.h) | `ClusterLegalizer` 与 `ClusterLegalizationStrategy` 枚举（合法化力度）。 |
| [vpr/src/base/vpr_types.h](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/base/vpr_types.h) | `e_cluster_seed` 枚举、`t_packer_opts` 中控制增益的各项权重。 |

---

## 4. 核心概念与源码讲解

### 4.1 贪心聚簇主循环

#### 4.1.1 概念说明

「贪心聚簇」的核心直觉很简单：**先把最难装的分子当种子装进一个空簇，再把和这个簇关系最铁的分子一个个吸进来，直到塞满。** 然后开下一个空簇，重复，直到所有分子都装完。

为什么「先装难装的」？因为一个使用了大量输入引脚的分子（比如一个大 LUT 或一段进位链）很难塞进一个已经半满的簇——簇内布线资源可能不够。所以让它当种子、从空簇开始装，成功率最高。这正是种子增益会考虑「使用的外部输入数」的原因（见 4.2）。

为什么「贪心」？因为每一步都只看局部最优（当前增益最高的候选），不去搜索全局最优组合——那是 NP 难问题。贪心换来的是速度：VPR 要在分钟级内处理成千上万的原子。

#### 4.1.2 核心流程

`do_clustering` 的主循环可以用下面伪代码概括：

```
构造 GreedyCandidateSelector（维护候选增益）
构造 GreedySeedSelector（预排序种子）
seed = seed_selector.get_next_seed()
while seed 有效:
    # 第一次尝试：跳过每步簇内布线（快但可能漏检非法）
    cluster = try_grow_cluster(seed, strategy = SKIP_INTRA_LB_ROUTE)
    if cluster 无效:
        # 第二次尝试：每加一个分子都做完整合法化（慢但保成功）
        cluster = try_grow_cluster(seed, strategy = FULL)
    assert cluster 有效            # 种子一定能成簇（否则直接致命报错）
    更新进度统计 / 打印进度
    seed = seed_selector.get_next_seed()
返回 各逻辑块类型的使用实例数
```

`try_grow_cluster` 单个簇的生长流程：

```
设置合法化策略 strategy
cluster_id = start_new_cluster(seed)            # 用种子开一个新簇，挑块类型/模式
gain_stats = candidate_selector.create_cluster_gain_stats(seed, cluster_id)
candidate = candidate_selector.get_next_candidate_for_cluster(gain_stats)
while candidate 有效 且 重复候选数 < 上限:
    success = try_add_candidate_mol_to_cluster(candidate, cluster_id)
    if success: 更新增益（成功）
    else:       更新增益（失败，记录失败次数）
    下一个 candidate
if 不是 ensure_legal_final_routing(cluster_id):  # 最终合法性兜底
    销毁非法簇，返回无效 id
finalize_cluster / clean_cluster
返回 cluster_id
```

两种合法化策略由枚举定义：

[cluster_legalizer.h:L66-L71](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/cluster_legalizer.h#L66-L71) —— `FULL` 每加一个分子都跑完整簇内布线；`SKIP_INTRA_LB_ROUTE` 跳过中间每步的簇内布线、只在最后统一做一次。后者更快，但如果最后的统一布线失败，整个簇作废，于是改用前者重试。

#### 4.1.3 源码精读

`do_clustering` 的初始化阶段先造好两个选择器，并取出第一个种子：

[greedy_clusterer.cpp:L130-L156](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_clusterer.cpp#L130-L156) —— 构造 `GreedyCandidateSelector` 与 `GreedySeedSelector`，然后 `seed_selector.get_next_seed()` 取出第一个种子。注意 `max_molecule_stats`（所有分子的最大统计值）被同时传给两个选择器，用于增益项的归一化。

主循环用「先快后慢」两段式尝试长簇：

[greedy_clusterer.cpp:L166-L208](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_clusterer.cpp#L166-L208) —— `while (seed_mol_id.is_valid())` 内：先用 `SKIP_INTRA_LB_ROUTE` 长簇；若返回无效 id，再用 `FULL` 重试；最后断言 `new_cluster_id.is_valid()` 与种子已被聚类。注释里的「基本算法」清楚写出了这种两段式思路。

`try_grow_cluster` 里关键的候选吸收循环：

[greedy_clusterer.cpp:L292-L329](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_clusterer.cpp#L292-L329) —— `while (candidate_mol_id.is_valid() && num_repeated_molecules < max_num_repeated_molecules)`：尝试把候选塞进簇，按成功/失败分别调用增益更新，再取下一个候选；若下一个候选和上一个相同，重复计数加一。正常情况下重复一次就停；但当吸引力组启用时，`max_num_repeated_molecules` 被放大到 500，让簇尽量塞满同组成员（见 4.4）。

簇长完后的合法性兜底：

[greedy_clusterer.cpp:L341-L350](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_clusterer.cpp#L341-L350) —— `ensure_legal_final_routing` 做最终的簇内布线检查；若失败，扣减类型计数、`destroy_cluster` + `compress`、返回无效 id，触发外层的 FULL 重试。

`start_new_cluster` 决定种子放进哪种逻辑块、哪个模式：

[greedy_clusterer.cpp:L463-L479](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_clusterer.cpp#L463-L479) —— 双层循环遍历候选逻辑块类型与该类型的各模式，调用 `cluster_legalizer.start_new_cluster(seed_mol_id, type, j)`，第一个通过的即采用；全部失败则 `VPR_FATAL_ERROR`（种子必须能成簇）。

#### 4.1.4 代码实践

**实践目标**：看清「一次聚簇迭代」中 `try_grow_cluster` 的两次调用与兜底逻辑。

**操作步骤**：

1. 打开 [greedy_clusterer.cpp:L166](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_clusterer.cpp#L166) 的 `while` 主循环。
2. 在第 179 行（第一次 `try_grow_cluster` 调用之后、`if (!new_cluster_id.is_valid())` 之前）和第 207 行（`VTR_ASSERT(new_cluster_id.is_valid())`）各加一行日志，例如：
   ```cpp
   VTR_LOG("DEBUG: seed %zu first_strategy_valid=%d\n", size_t(seed_mol_id), new_cluster_id.is_valid());
   ```
   （示例代码，仅供本地观察，不要提交。）
3. 用 `make -j8 vpr` 重新编译，再跑一个小的打包（如 `run_vtr_flow.py` 跑一个 blink 设计，见 u1-l4）。

**需要观察的现象**：日志会显示对每个种子，`first_strategy_valid` 大多数时候为 1（即快的策略就够），偶尔为 0（触发慢的 FULL 重试）。

**预期结果**：绝大多数种子第一次（跳过簇内布线）就能成簇；只有少数布局拥挤的种子需要第二次完整合法化。这印证了「先快后慢」的设计动机。

**待本地验证**：若你暂时无法编译，可改为纯阅读实践——通读 `try_grow_cluster`（L235–L367），用自己的话写出「候选循环何时退出」的三种条件。

#### 4.1.5 小练习与答案

**练习 1**：为什么 `do_clustering` 要先用 `SKIP_INTRA_LB_ROUTE` 再用 `FULL`，而不是一开始就用 `FULL`？

**参考答案**：簇内布线（intra-lb route）是合法化里最贵的一步。先跳过它、只在最后统一检查一次，能在大多数簇上省掉大量中间布线开销；只有最后检查失败时才退回到每步都做完整布线的 FULL 策略。这是「快路径优先、慢路径兜底」的经典优化。

**练习 2**：主循环里 `VTR_ASSERT(new_cluster_id.is_valid())`（[L207](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_clusterer.cpp#L207)）为什么一定成立？万一不成立会怎样？

**参考答案**：因为 `start_new_cluster` 保证种子一定能放进某个逻辑块（全部失败会直接 `VPR_FATAL_ERROR` 终止程序），而 FULL 策略下每加一个分子都做完整合法化，注释明确说「这不可能失败」。所以两次尝试后簇必然有效；若断言失败，说明出现了未预期的架构/算法 bug。

### 4.2 种子选择器 GreedySeedSelector

#### 4.2.1 概念说明

聚簇是「一个簇一个簇地造」，**先造哪个分子当种子，会显著影响最终聚类质量**。`GreedySeedSelector` 的职责就是：在聚簇开始前，给每个分子算一个「种子增益」，按增益从高到低排好序；之后每次被要种子时，就按顺序返回下一个还没被聚类的分子。

它的设计有两个关键点：

- **预计算 + 排序**：增益在构造函数里一次性算好并 `stable_sort`，之后 `get_next_seed()` 只是顺序遍历，非常快（O(1) 摊还）。
- **不重复、不返已聚类**：它维护一个游标 `seed_index_`，假定「一旦分子被聚类就再也不会被取消聚类」，因此只需跳过已被聚类的，不会回头。

#### 4.2.2 核心流程

```
构造时：
  seed_mols_ = 全部分子
  若是 timing_driven：算每个原子的 criticality（关键度）
  for 每个分子 mol：
      mol_gain = max(它的每个原子的 get_seed_gain(...))   # 分子增益取其原子增益的最大值
  stable_sort(seed_mols_, 按增益降序)
  若有 RAM 组：stable_partition 把 RAM 种子挪到最前
  seed_index_ = 0

get_next_seed():
  while seed_index_ < seed_mols_.size():
      mol = seed_mols_[seed_index_++]
      if mol 未被聚类: return mol
  return INVALID    # 全部聚类完毕
```

种子增益由 `e_cluster_seed` 枚举控制（默认 `blend2`，见 vpr_types.h 注释）：

[vpr_types.h:L150-L155](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/base/vpr_types.h#L150-L155) —— `TIMING`、`MAX_INPUTS`、`BLEND`、`MAX_PINS`、`MAX_INPUT_PINS`、`BLEND2` 等多种选种策略。

以 `BLEND` 为例，其种子增益是一个加权和，并用分子包含的块数做放大：

\[
\text{blend\_gain} = \big(0.5 \cdot \text{criticality} + 0.5 \cdot \frac{\text{num\_used\_ext\_inputs}}{\max\_\text{num\_used\_ext\_inputs}}\big) \times \big(1 + 0.2 \cdot (\text{num\_blocks} - 1)\big)
\]

直觉：关键度高（timing critical）的分子优先当种子，有助于时序；用的外部输入越多越优先当种子，因为这些分子最难塞进半满的簇；分子越大（`num_blocks` 越多，如长进位链）越优先。

#### 4.2.3 源码精读

种子增益的核心计算（`get_seed_gain`）：

[greedy_seed_selector.cpp:L34-L128](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_seed_selector.cpp#L34-L128) —— 一个 `switch` 按 `seed_type` 分派：`TIMING` 直接返回原子关键度；`MAX_INPUTS` 返回分子使用的外部输入数；`BLEND` 是上面公式（L57-L72）；`BLEND2`（L89-L123）是更精细的多项加权和，用一组固定权重（`INPUT_PIN_WEIGHT=0.5`、`USED_INPUT_PIN_WEIGHT=0.2`、`BLOCKS_WEIGHT=0.2`、`CRITICALITY_WEIGHT=0.1`）组合多个归一化比值。注释里都写明了每种策略的「直觉」。

构造函数里给分子打分并稳定排序：

[greedy_seed_selector.cpp:L202-L234](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_seed_selector.cpp#L202-L234) —— 对每个分子，遍历其原子取 `get_seed_gain` 的最大值作为分子增益；随后用 `std::stable_sort` 按增益降序。注释特别强调用 *stable*（稳定）排序：不同标准库的 `std::sort` 对等增益元素的顺序未定义，会导致不同编译器下种子顺序不同、结果不可复现。

RAM 种子前置：

[greedy_seed_selector.cpp:L240-L247](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_seed_selector.cpp#L240-L247) —— 若有 RAM 组，用 `std::stable_partition` 把属于 RAM 组的种子挪到最前，让 RAM 簇先成形，从而给后续非 RAM 簇提供更完整的连通性信息。

`get_next_seed` 的顺序遍历与跳过：

[greedy_seed_selector.cpp:L261-L282](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_seed_selector.cpp#L261-L282) —— 游标 `seed_index_` 单调递增，遇到已被聚类的就 `continue` 跳过，否则返回；越界返回 `INVALID`。注释点明了核心假设：分子一旦被聚类就永不取消。

#### 4.2.4 代码实践

**实践目标**：理解 `get_next_seed` 为何是 O(1) 摊还，以及 RAM 种子前置的效果。

**操作步骤**：

1. 阅读 [greedy_seed_selector.cpp:L261-L282](https://github.com/verilog-to-routing/vtr-verilog-to-routing/blob/c3ad1ec64818c9db312e24967de9d6283410ab19/vpr/src/pack/greedy_seed_selector.cpp#L261-L282)。
2. 回答：如果允许「分子被聚类后又取消聚类」，这段代码会出什么问题？（提示：游标 `seed_index_` 只前进。）
3. 阅读构造函数末尾对 `seed_index_ = 0` 的赋值，确认第一个被提出的种子就是增益最高的分子。

**需要观察的现象**（纯阅读型）：游标只会前进、从不回退；被跳过的只是「已被聚类」的，不是「曾被提出」的——因为提出后往往紧接着就被聚类了。

**预期结果**：你会确认 `get_next_seed` 的总工作量为 O(分子数)，分摊到每次调用是 O(1)。

#### 4.2.5 小练习与答案

**练习 1**：`BLEND` 种子增益里乘以 \((1 + 0.2(\text{num\_blocks}-1))\) 这一项的目的是什么？

**参考答案**：放大含多个原子的大分子（如长进位链分子）的种子增益，让它们更优先当种子。大分子最难塞进半满的簇，让它们从空簇开始装能提高成功率与聚类质量。

**练习 2**：为什么分子增益取其「所有原子增益的最大值」而不是平均值？

**参考答案**：分子的「难度/关键性」由它最难处理或最关键的原子决定。取最大值确保只要分子里有一个高增益原子，整个分子就被优先当种子，避免把含有进位链头等关键结构的分子埋在后面。
